title: Unexpected Return
content:
It's been a while since the last post. A lot has unfolded, and I thought it's about time for an update.

Lately, my work life has taken an interesting turn. I've found myself diving back into the world of programming—a realm I didn't expect to revisit with such intensity. In fact, I'm now spending about one-third of my working hours writing code to analyze data. Ironically, this was the aspect I once aimed to steer clear of, yet it’s turned into a rewarding shift, infusing unexpected joy and fulfillment into my daily routine.

The next third of my time has taken an unexpected turn towards communicating with clients. Navigating between the analytical tasks and these interpersonal interactions has been a challenge, and honestly, it's not the part I enjoy the most.

Outside of work, I've embraced the art of doing not much at all, especially on the weekends. It's become a cherished time to recharge, happily spent without a packed schedule or pressing commitments. Whether it's lounging at home, indulging in hobbies that I've neglected, or simply enjoying the quiet moments of reflection, these periods of downtime have become a sanctuary of sorts.

I've always been someone who preferred little change, and now, finding comfort in less excitement has become my new normal. It's a simple shift in perspective, but it's made all the difference.
